name: New-PSScriptFileInfo

options.name: -Author
options.description: The name of the author of the script.

options.name: -CompanyName
options.description: The name of the company owning the script.

options.name: -Copyright
options.description: The copyright information for the script.

options.name: -ExternalModuleDependencies
options.description: The list of external module dependencies taken by this script.

options.name: -ExternalScriptDependencies
options.description: The list of external script dependencies taken by this script.

options.name: -Force
options.description: Forces the cmdlet to overwrite any existing file.

options.name: -Guid
options.description: The unique identifier for the script in GUID format. If you don't provide a GUID, the cmdlet creates a new one automatically.

options.name: -IconUri
options.description: A Uniform Resource Identifier (URI) pointing to the icon associated with the script.

options.name: -LicenseUri
options.description: The URI pointing to the license agreement file associated with the script.

options.name: -Path
options.description: The filename and location where the script is created.

options.name: -PrivateData
options.description: The private data associated with the script.

options.name: -ProjectUri
options.description: The URI pointing to the project site associated with the script.

options.name: -ReleaseNotes
options.description: The release notes for the script.

options.name: -RequiredModules
options.description: The parameter takes an array of module specification hashtables. A module specification is a hashtable that has the following keys.

options.name: -RequiredScripts
options.description: The list of scripts required by the script.

name: -Tags
-Tag
options.description: The tags associated with the script. Tag values are strings that shouldn't contain spaces. For more information, see Tag details (/powershell/scripting/gallery/concepts/package-manifest-affecting-ui#tag-details).

options.name: -Version
options.description: The version of the script. If no value is provided Version defaults to `1.0.0.0`.

description: The cmdlet creates a new script file, including metadata about the script.
